title: podman fails to run a container if TMPDIR directory does not exist
body:
### Issue Description

I got a new laptop and didn't copy `~/tmp` over from my old laptop.

However upon using my old bash config which sets TMPDIR to the expansion of ~/tmp
podman stopped working, since I hadn't created ~/tmp.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `TMPDIR=/somewhere/nonexistent podman run fedora`


### Describe the results you received

```
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Error: initializing destination containers-storage:[overlay@/home/pete/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000/containers]registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest: creating a temporary directory: stat /somewhere/nonexistent: no such file or directory
```

### Describe the results you expected

Well I admit this is probably a corner case and I guess the error is not terrible: but the problem is toolbox for example failed silently basically - could argue then it is a toolbox issue, but I also feel podman could surface the issue better to the user

Perhaps it would be better to fallback to /tmp say if the TMPDIR dir doesn't exist?

comments.body:
Hi @juhp 

Thank you for the bug report. I personally don't think we should change this behavior and the error message is quite clear.

if you haven't I would suggest you raise this issue with the toolbox project as if it's silently failing that isn't a very good UX.
I agree silently ignoring TMPDIR seems much worse as it would actively hide this misconfiguration
@debarshiray should we move this to toolbx? Or I can open a new issue: though I don't have any new/further info to add

Yeah, Toolbx could sanity check the `TMPDIR` environment variable if it's set on start-up.

Catching the exit code of `podman exec` is tricky because it can be the exit code of whatever was running inside the CLI shell started by it.  eg., `exit 3`.

The few times I tried this, I almost always got this from `podman exec`, which is visible with `toolbox --verbose enter`:
```
Error: container create failed (no logs from conmon): conmon bytes "": readObjectStart: expect { or n, but found , error found in #0 byte of ...||..., bigger context ...||...
```

On one occasion, `toolbox(1)` failed to detect that the container was started and the entry point was ready.  I have no idea why and I can't reproduce that again.
